perturbation.original_text: 3. Except as otherwise permitted by this Agreement, the receiving party agrees that it shall not copy, adapt, divulge, publish, disclose, or circulate (or authorize or permit anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate) any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party (except as described in paragraph 4), nor shall it use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The receiving party shall make reasonable efforts to avoid copying, adapting, divulging, publishing, disclosing, or circulating (or authorizing or permitting anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate) any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party (except as described in paragraph 4), and may use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Opportunity with the disclosing party's consent. In the absence of an explicit written agreement stating otherwise, the Receiving party remains fully liable in case Confidential Information is divulged, published, disclosed or circulated.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'shall not' is replaced with 'shall make reasonable efforts to avoid', weakening the restriction on use and disclosure, creating ambiguity and weakening the original provision, due to its vagueness on what is consider "reasonable efforts". The addition, to use Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Opportunity with the disclosing party's consent, introduce an In-Text Contradiction with what is expressed in the first part of the same paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The receiving party shall make reasonable efforts to avoid copying, adapting, divulging, publishing, disclosing, or circulating (or authorizing or permitting anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate) any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party (except as described in paragraph 4), and may use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Opportunity with the disclosing party's consent. In the absence of an explicit written agreement stating otherwise, the Receiving party remains fully liable in case Confidential Information is divulged, published, disclosed or circulated.
perturbation.justification: The original text in paragraph 3 strictly prohibits the receiving party from using Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Opportunity. The changed text weakens this restriction by allowing the receiving party to use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Opportunity with the disclosing party's consent. This directly contradicts the initial prohibition, creating a clear and direct conflict within the same paragraph. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text. The Change creates a contradiction with what is expressed in the first part of the same paragraph

perturbation.original_text: 7. The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity. The receiving party hereby agrees that all Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) disclosed to or created by the receiving party shall be delivered forthwith by the receiving party to the disclosing party, or, at the election of the receiving party, destroyed, and deleted from all retrieval systems and data bases upon the earlier of a request at any time by the disclosing party or at any time the receiving party decides not to proceed with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity. Unless otherwise stated, the receiving party may retain copies of the Confidential Information for archival purposes even after the Opportunity concludes.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds 'Unless otherwise stated, the receiving party may retain copies of the Confidential Information for archival purposes even after the Opportunity concludes,' directly contradicting the original requirement to return or destroy all Confidential Information at the end of the Opportunity, creating an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity. Unless otherwise stated, the receiving party may retain copies of the Confidential Information for archival purposes even after the Opportunity concludes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing the receiving party to retain copies of the Confidential Information for archival purposes after the Opportunity concludes. This directly contradicts the original text's requirement that all Confidential Information (including copies) must be returned or destroyed at the end of the Opportunity. Therefore, this introduces a strong in-text contradiction as it creates two mutually exclusive obligations regarding the disposal of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The disclosing party shall at its discretion provide such of the Confidential Information to the receiving party as is necessary or appropriate in connection with the Opportunity. Nothing in this Agreement obligates the disclosing party to make any particular disclosure of Confidential Information. The receiving party acknowledges and agrees that no representations or warranties whatsoever, express Page 2 ​ of 4 or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information have been made by the disclosing party. The receiving party acknowledges and agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives or professional advisors will have any NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT liability to the receiving party resulting from its use of the Confidential Information or from any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information. Only those representations and warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the proposed transaction with the disclosing party, when, as and if executed, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The disclosing party shall provide all Confidential Information to the receiving party that the receiving party deems necessary for the Opportunity. The disclosing party warrants that all Confidential Information provided is accurate and complete to the best of its knowledge. The receiving party acknowledges and agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives or professional advisors will have any NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT liability to the receiving party resulting from its use of the Confidential Information or from any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information. Only those representations and warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the proposed transaction with the disclosing party, when, as and if executed, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: This edit makes the disclosing party responsible to give all confidential information. This directly introduces an In-Text Contradiction, on what it should be done, by the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The disclosing party shall provide all Confidential Information to the receiving party that the receiving party deems necessary for the Opportunity. The disclosing party warrants that all Confidential Information provided is accurate and complete to the best of its knowledge. The receiving party acknowledges and agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives or professional advisors will have any NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT liability to the receiving party resulting from its use of the Confidential Information or from any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information. Only those representations and warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the proposed transaction with the disclosing party, when, as and if executed, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the disclosing party will provide confidential information at its discretion, without obligation. The changed text makes it mandatory for the disclosing party to provide all confidential information the receiving party deems necessary and warrants its accuracy. This directly contradicts the original discretionary provision and introduces a warranty where none existed before, which is not aligned with the contradicted section, thus a strong contradiction exists. The changed text's warranty also contradicts the liability disclaimer.

perturbation.original_text: 11. The receiving party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the disclosing party and its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives and professional advisors from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including reasonable legal fees and the cost of enforcing this Agreement) owed to a third party arising out of the breach by the receiving party of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. The receiving party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the disclosing party and its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives and professional advisors from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including reasonable legal fees and the cost of enforcing this Agreement) owed to a third party arising out of the gross negligence or willful misconduct by the receiving party of this Agreement. However, in the case that Confidential Information, already in the public domain prior to the disclosure, is divulged the Indemnification clause in clause 11 will be fully dismissed.
perturbation.explanation: This edit introduces a condition of 'gross negligence or willful misconduct' modifying the original clause, and adds the disclaimer on Confidential Information. This introduces a direct In-Text Contradiction with the original clause, because the receiving party is not responsible in certain scenario, and in the other, the receiving party is fully responsible, and without exceptions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. The receiving party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the disclosing party and its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives and professional advisors from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including reasonable legal fees and the cost of enforcing this Agreement) owed to a third party arising out of the gross negligence or willful misconduct by the receiving party of this Agreement. However, in the case that Confidential Information, already in the public domain prior to the disclosure, is divulged the Indemnification clause in clause 11 will be fully dismissed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction. Originally, clause 11 provides full indemnification by the receiving party for any breach. The modified text limits this indemnification to 'gross negligence or willful misconduct' and adds an exception for confidential information already in the public domain. This directly contradicts the broader indemnification originally stated. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this scenario, making the contradiction clear, as the original clause now has exceptions that create in-text contradiction.
